[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request whose req.baseUrl contains path-traversal characters so that the constructed res.locals[requestPathLabel] matches a different entry in headers.path[], causing the responseHandler to apply a longer cache-control TTL to fee-bearing transaction responses and serve stale charged_tx_fee values to subsequent users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the !res.locals[requestPathLabel] guard in recordRequestPath so that a prior middleware that sets requestPathLabel to a fee-unrelated path causes the responseHandler to select wrong per-path cache headers for a transaction fee endpoint, making charged_tx_fee data appear fresher or staler than it actually is?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the router.useExt(recordRequestPath) registration on all three routers (AccountRoutes, BlockRoutes, ContractRoutes) by sending a request that matches multiple routers in sequence, causing recordRequestPath to be invoked multiple times and the first invocation to lock in a wrong requestPathLabel that suppresses correct fee-related cache headers on the final response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with URL-encoded path separators (%2F) in the contractId parameter so that req.baseUrl + req.route.path in recordRequestPath produces a path containing double slashes, which after .replace(/\\/+$/g, '') normalization matches an unintended headers.path[] key and applies wrong cache-control headers to responses containing max_fee_per_gas and max_priority_fee_per_gas?
